strategy: She punishes almost every action, so keep your deck lean: fewer, bigger plays beat lots of small ones.
Damaging her triggers a Moon Insane Bolt AoE (random 550, 1,050, or 1,550) on your team, so shield the group and be ready to heal after each hit.
Skip DoTs (she counters with Kraken), damage debuffs (Hammer of Thor), and extra Feints (Insane Bolt twice); each just eats a punish.
Blades and Globals trigger an AoE Enfeeble or a self-heal via Healing Current. With 45% incoming healing she heals well, so bring Doom and Gloom or infection and blade the same round you hit.
She has no boost school and 20% resist to everything, so attack with your strongest school, stack a couple of blades, and burst her down accepting the Insane Bolt return.
cheatNotes: Arriving late gets your whole team hit with a Moon Insane Bolt (550, 1,050, or 1,550). Join on time.
If she has a Heal-over-Time ticking at end of round she casts Thundering Jinn, so do not hand her healing to protect.
Any damage on her returns a Moon Insane Bolt AoE on your team; shield before you hit.
A DoT on her triggers Kraken, so leave DoTs out.
Blades and Globals make her cast an AoE Enfeeble or heal herself with Healing Current. Blade the same round you hit and pack an infection.
A damage debuff (weakness type) triggers Hammer of Thor, so do not debuff her damage.
Feinting her triggers Insane Bolt plus a Moon Insane Bolt AoE, the priciest punish. Avoid Feint.
Casting Dissipate triggers another Moon Insane Bolt AoE.
